Skip to contents
fishr 0.1.0
  • Reference

Resolve fish2 body ids from a variety of inputs

fish_ids.Rd

Extracts body ids from vectors, data.frames, or query strings. Query strings are passed to malevnc::manc_ids via the fish2 neuprint server. For filtering DVID annotations by field, see fish_dvid_annotations.

Usage

fish_ids(
  x,
  mustWork = TRUE,
  as_character = TRUE,
  integer64 = FALSE,
  unique = TRUE,
  conn = NULL
)

Arguments

x

A numeric/character vector of body ids, a data.frame with a bodyid column, or a neuprint query string (e.g. "DNa02", "where:status='Traced'").

mustWork

If TRUE (default) an error is raised when no ids are found.

as_character

Whether to return ids as character (default TRUE). Character is the safest default because body ids may exceed the 53-bit integer limit of double-precision floating point (2^53 - 1).

integer64

Whether to return ids as bit64::integer64 (default FALSE). Takes precedence over as_character.

unique

Whether to de-duplicate the result (default TRUE).

conn

A neuprint_connection object (default NULL uses fish_neuprint).

Value

A character vector of body ids (default), or bit64::integer64 when integer64=TRUE or as_character=FALSE.

See also

Other fishr-package: fish_dvid_annotations(), fish_setup(), read_fish_meshes(), read_fish_neurons(), with_fish()

Examples

# \donttest{
# neuprint query by type
fish_ids("RGC")
#>    [1] "111554667" "111584814" "111627703" "111636789" "111647724" "111656787"
#>    [7] "100009896" "100015071" "100015468" "100017513" "100023948" "100061403"
#>   [13] "100068896" "100079855" "100106910" "100109349" "100129744" "100244865"
#>   [19] "100258917" "100265082" "100274961" "100296057" "100296109" "100301144"
#>   [25] "100302578" "100315174" "100351203" "100355364" "100376471" "100501734"
#>   [31] "100512497" "100514766" "100528404" "100529149" "100533827" "100539938"
#>   [37] "100556441" "100563082" "100570048" "100572418" "100573438" "100575537"
#>   [43] "100582959" "100593814" "100606131" "100606567" "100632285" "100634671"
#>   [49] "100747037" "100747687" "100748932" "100749883" "100753651" "100789143"
#>   [55] "100791668" "100802180" "100806646" "100813654" "100824355" "100836202"
#>   [61] "100836240" "100838367" "100844215" "100848522" "100854727" "100863783"
